summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 14: re-detect against main (80 framework bugs fixed across rounds 0-13). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (86 sigs), adversarial verify. Round 13 found 5 (versioning cast-corruption HIGH, command-palette path, widgets SelectFilter multiple, audit morph-map, auth password-broker) so the consecutive-clean counter is 0/2. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 14 detection: 2 from clusters = 2 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 14 verify: 2 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Form validation rules ignore record-dependent layout visibility (visibleIf/canSee) — a required field hidden by an enclosing layout for THIS record is still validated as required, producing an unsubmittable update form
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:590 (extractRules calls $resource->effectiveFields() with no record); also :382/:380 validated() does not thread the record
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Thread the loaded record through validated()/extractRules() and call $resource->effectiveFields($record) for rule extraction (record is available on update at ResourceController::update line 104; pass null on create to match the render path).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced and confirmed. The render path (InertiaDataBuilder::buildFormPayload, lines 321/325: effectiveFields($record) + form->toArray($record)) and the write-prune path (ResourceController::pruneUnauthorizedFields, line 471: effectiveFields($record)) are both record-aware and correctly drop a field enclosed by a layout Section/Tab/Group whose visibleIf/canSee predicate evaluates false for the current record. But the validation-rules path is NOT: validated() (line 380) calls extractRules() (line 382) which calls $resource->effectiveFields() (line 590) with NO record argument. Form::flatten then evaluates layout isVisibleFor(null); a record-dependent predicate such as Section::visibleIf(fn($r)=>$r?->getAttribute('locked')!==true) is true for null, so the enclosed field's rules (including required) are emitted. Direct reproduction against apps/showcase autoloader with Arqel\\Fields\\Types\\TextField + Arqel\\Form\\Form + Arqel\\Form\\Layout\\Section + FieldRulesExtractor: getFields(null)=>['name','secret_code'], getFields(locked)=>['name'], extract(null)=>{name:[required],secret_code:[required]}, extract(locked)=>{name:[required]}. So on update of a locked record the field is never rendered yet validation requires it. Since validated() (update line 108) runs $request->validate() BEFORE pruneUnauthorizedFields (line 109), the submit fails with a 422 'secret_code required' and the form is unsubmittable; even supplying the value would have it dropped by the prune step. Confirmed the existing LayoutWriteVisibilityTest only exercises the write-prune path with rule-less stub fields (LayoutVisField has no getValidationRules), so the validation path for a layout-hidden required field is genuinely untested. Distinct entry point from the Round-6 #115 fix which only wired layout visibility into render serialization and write-prune. Framework-bug: visibleIf/canSee on layout components is documented public API supported on every other path. Severity medium: requires an app to use a record-dependent layout predicate guarding a required field (not present in the current showcase, so not a baseline regression), but it is a fully supported config that yields a hard unrecoverable block.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: ResourceController::extractRules() (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:590) builds the validation rule set via $resource->effectiveFields() with NO record argument. effectiveFields(?Model $record = null) (packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:174-187) delegates to Form::getFields($record) (packages/form/src/Form.php:125-158), which prunes fields enclosed by a layout Component whose isVisibleFor($record) is false. Because extractRules passes null, Form::flatten evaluates Section/Tab/Group visibleIf/canSee against a null record, so a layout whose predicate is record-dependent (e.g. Section::visibleIf(fn($r)=>$r?->getAttribute('locked') !== true), true for null) is treated as VISIBLE and its child field's rules (including 'required') are emitted. Meanwhile the render path (buildEditData -> effectiveFields($record) + Form::toArray($record)) AND the write-prune path (pruneUnauthorizedFields, ResourceController.php:471, passes $record) both correctly prune that same field for the actual record. Net asymmetry on update of a record for which the layout is hidden: the form never shows the field, the user cannot supply it, yet request->validate() rejects with 'the secret code field is required' -> the form is unsubmittable; and even if the value were supplied, pruneUnauthorizedFields drops it (it is absent from effectiveFields($record)). Verified by direct reproduction against the showcase autoloader using Arqel\Fields\Types\TextField + Arqel\Form\Form + Arqel\Form\FieldRulesExtractor + Arqel\Form\Layout\Section: for a locked record, getFields($record) => ['name'] (field pruned at render) while getFields(null) => ['name','secret_code'] and FieldRulesExtractor::extract() => {"name":["required"],"secret_code":["required"]} — required at validation, hidden at render. This is a DISTINCT entry point from the Round-6 #115 fix, which wired layout canSee/visibleIf only into the render serialization and the write-prune path; the validation-rules path (extractRules) was never made record-aware. Fix: thread the loaded record into extractRules()/validated() and call effectiveFields($record) for rule extraction (record is available on update at ResourceController::update line 104; on create record is null, matching the render). No existing test covers layout-hidden field validation (only InertiaValidationFlowTest covers the fail-closed null/extractor-absent guard).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: WidgetRenderer.withFilters() mangles object/array filter values (date_range, multiple-select) on deferred/poll refetch — String(value) yields '[object Object]' / comma-joined string
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In withFilters(), recurse on value type instead of a flat String(value): for arrays append `filters[${key}][]` per element; for plain objects append `filters[${key}][${subKey}]` per entry (skipping null/undefined/'' leaves); keep scalar branch as-is. This matches Laravel's nested-array parsing of `$request->input('filters')` and the SSR/Inertia qs serialization.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed by inspection of the full live data flow, not refuted. WidgetRenderer.tsx:135 builds the deferred/poll refetch URL with `params.append(\`filters[${key}]\`, String(value))`, assuming scalar values. But the dashboard filter system supplies non-scalar values: DateRangeControl (DashboardFilters.tsx:182,190) emits a `{from,to}` object and multiple SelectControl (DashboardFilters.tsx:124, multiple-select path fixed Round-13) emits a `string[]`. `String({from,to})` is the literal `"[object Object]"`; `String(['a','b'])` is `"a,b"` sent as one flat param instead of `filters[k][]=a&filters[k][]=b`. These values reach withFilters live: DashboardFilters.onChange -> ArqelDashboardPage.handleFilterChange -> filterValues -> DashboardGrid (WidgetRenderer filterValues={values}) -> filterRef.current -> withFilters (WidgetRenderer.tsx:72). The server WidgetDataController.php:64-67 reads `$request->input('filters')` as a nested array and seeds `$widget->filters(array_merge(defaults, requestFilters))`, so `filterValue('range')` returns the corrupted string rather than `{from,to}`/the array. Asymmetry confirms the defect is isolated to the JS URL builder: the SSR/deep-link path uses `router.get(path, {filters: next})` (ArqelDashboardPage.tsx:42) which Inertia/qs serializes into proper bracket notation and Laravel parses into nested arrays — so a deferred/polling widget's first lazy fetch silently disagrees with its SSR payload. Classification is framework-bug (not app-misuse): the app never constructs this URL; withFilters is framework-owned code in @arqel-dev/ui. Not a documented/deferred feature. The only existing test (WidgetRenderer.test.tsx:178) asserts a scalar (`filters%5Bstatus%5D=published`); object/array filter values are entirely uncovered, masking the gap. Severity medium: it corrupts refetches only for the two non-scalar filter types and only on deferred/poll fetches (initial SSR render is correct), so it degrades silently rather than crashing.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages-js/ui/src/widgets/WidgetRenderer.tsx line 135: `params.append(\`filters[${key}]\`, String(value));` inside `withFilters()` (line 129). This is the URL builder used by the deferred + polling widget refetch path (called at line 72 with the live `DashboardFilters` selection). It assumes every filter value is a scalar. But the dashboard filter system explicitly supports non-scalar values: (1) `DateRangeFilter` (packages/widgets/src/Filters/DateRangeFilter.php) whose value is an object `{from, to}` — `String({from,to})` produces the literal string `"[object Object]"`, so the server receives `filters[range]=[object Object]`; (2) `SelectFilter::multiple()` (packages/widgets/src/Filters/SelectFilter.php, render path fixed Round-13 #192 so the JS value is an array) — `String(['a','b'])` produces `"a,b"`, sent as one flat param instead of `filters[k][]=a&filters[k][]=b`. The server `WidgetDataController` (packages/widgets/src/Http/Controllers/WidgetDataController.php) reads `$request->input('filters')` as a nested array and seeds it into `$widget->filters(...)`, so `Widget::filterValue('range')` returns the garbage string instead of `{from,to}` / the array. ASYMMETRY confirms the bug: the SSR / deep-link path (`?filters[range][from]=..&filters[range][to]=..` parsed by Laravel into a nested array, DashboardController passes through `filterValues`) works correctly — only the JS refetch URL builder is wrong, so a deferred/polling widget's first lazy fetch silently disagrees with its SSR payload. The only existing test (packages-js/ui/tests/widgets/WidgetRenderer.test.tsx line 178) asserts a SCALAR value (`filters%5Bstatus%5D=published`); object/array filter values are entirely uncovered. DashboardFilters.tsx already emits the array value (multiple SelectControl, line ~117) and the object value (DateRangeControl `onChange({from,to})`), so both reach withFilters live. Fix: serialize nested keys (e.g. `filters[k][from]`, `filters[k][]`) instead of a flat String(value).
